configuration.title: Broker Configuration
configuration.description: Configure broker control settings for this tenant.
configuration.limited_access_body: Brokers can save operational thresholds and review settings here. Tenant-wide policy toggles are visible for context but must be changed by an admin.
configuration.field_broker_messaging_enabled_help: Allow members to send messages directly to brokers.
configuration.field_broker_copy_all_messages_help: Send a broker copy of every member-to-member message.
configuration.field_copy_first_contact_help: Send a broker copy of the first message between any two members.
configuration.field_copy_new_member_messages_help: Send a broker copy of messages from members within their initial monitoring window.
configuration.field_copy_high_risk_listing_messages_he: Send a broker copy of messages related to listings tagged as high risk.
configuration.field_broker_copy_threshold_hours_help: Only copy messages tied to exchanges above this hours threshold.
configuration.field_broker_visible_to_members_help: Members see that broker oversight is active for this community.
configuration.field_show_broker_name_help: Display the assigned broker's name to members.
configuration.field_broker_contact_email_help: Public contact email for broker enquiries.
configuration.field_broker_approval_required_help: Require broker approval before exchanges can proceed.
configuration.field_require_exchange_for_listings_help: Members must create an exchange to engage with a listing.
configuration.field_require_approval_high_risk_help: Always require broker approval for exchanges flagged as high risk.
configuration.field_auto_approve_low_risk_help: Skip broker approval for exchanges classified as low risk.
configuration.field_auto_flag_high_risk_help: Automatically flag exchanges classified as high risk for broker review.
configuration.field_allow_hour_adjustment_help: Brokers can adjust the hours on an exchange before approval.
configuration.field_max_hour_variance_percent_help: Maximum percentage by which a broker can adjust the hours on an exchange.
configuration.field_max_hours_without_approval_help: Exchanges above this number of hours always require broker approval.
configuration.field_confirmation_deadline_hours_help: Hours allowed before an unconfirmed exchange auto-closes.
configuration.field_exchange_timeout_days_help: Days an exchange remains open before being marked timed-out.
configuration.field_expiry_hours_help: Hours a broker approval remains valid before requiring renewal.
configuration.field_vetting_enabled_help: Track DBS / Garda vetting records for members.
configuration.field_enforce_vetting_on_exchanges_help: Block exchanges where the provider lacks valid vetting.
configuration.field_enforce_insurance_on_exchanges_hel: Block exchanges where the provider lacks valid insurance.
configuration.field_new_member_monitoring_days_help: Number of days new members are monitored automatically.
configuration.field_random_sample_percentage_help: Percentage of messages to randomly copy to the broker for review.
configuration.field_retention_days_help: Days to retain broker archive records before purging.
configuration.field_risk_tagging_enabled_help: Allow brokers to tag listings with risk classifications.
configuration.field_notify_on_high_risk_match_help: Send an alert when a member engages with a listing tagged as high risk.
configuration.section_messaging_desc: How members reach brokers and which conversations enter the review queue.
configuration.section_risk_tagging_desc: Automatic risk classification and the alerts it raises for broker review.
configuration.section_exchange_workflow_desc: Approval rules, deadlines, and hour adjustments for exchanges.
configuration.section_broker_visibility_desc: What members can see about broker oversight in this community.
configuration.section_message_copy_rules_desc: Which member messages are copied to the broker for review.
configuration.section_compliance_safeguarding_desc: Vetting and insurance tracking, enforcement, and expiry warnings.
configuration.admin_only_tooltip: Only tenant admins can change this policy setting.
configuration.load_error_hint: Try again — if it keeps failing, the API may be unavailable.
configuration.field_vetting_expiry_warning_days_help: Days before a vetting record expires that expiry warnings begin.

help.title: Guidance & reference
help.subtitle: How the brokering and safeguarding system works — for admins and coordinators. Each section is independent — open whichever you need.

overview.title: What this module manages
overview.intro: Broker Controls is the operational hub for coordinators, brokers, and admins who mediate between members when safeguarding or compliance concerns are in play. It brings together six sub-modules:
overview.bullet_exchange: Exchange management — approve or reject exchanges that need broker sign-off.
overview.bullet_risk_tags: Risk tags — manually flag listings you want to watch.
overview.bullet_message_review: Message review — triage messages that have been copied to the broker queue.
overview.bullet_user_monitoring: User monitoring — put a member's communications under broker oversight.
overview.bullet_attestations: Contact attestations — record a community safeguarding decision without collecting certificates or disclosure details.
overview.bullet_configuration: Configuration — tune thresholds for how the system decides what to copy.
overview.access_note: Access is restricted to users with the admin, tenant_admin, broker, or super_admin role. Every view and action is written to the audit log.

workflow.title: Your daily workflow
workflow.intro: A recommended daily check-in routine for coordinators:
workflow.step_unreviewed: Unreviewed messages — open Message Review and clear anything flagged red (high severity) first. Aim for a 24-hour SLA on flagged items.
workflow.step_pending: Pending exchanges — approve or reject exchanges awaiting broker sign-off. An unapproved exchange blocks both parties.
workflow.step_alerts: Safeguarding alerts — if the red counter on the safeguarding tile is non-zero, jump to the Safeguarding dashboard to triage.
workflow.step_vetting_reviews: Vetting review requests — decide whether the community can confirm its fixed contact attestation, or close the request without collecting documents.
workflow.step_activity: Recent activity feed — skim the activity list to catch patterns: repeat senders, unusual volumes, new monitored users.
workflow.tip: The stat tiles all deep-link into the correct filter — one click from overview to actionable list.

messages.title: How message review works
messages.intro: When a member is under monitoring (either self-declared during onboarding or placed under monitoring by an admin), the system automatically copies each message they send or receive into a separate broker review queue. The original message is delivered normally — members never know a copy was made unless you tell them.
messages.severity_high: High — flagged by the content scanner for keywords or patterns of concern.
messages.severity_medium: Medium — sender or recipient is a flagged user (no content match).
messages.severity_low: Low — routine copy for audit; no automatic concerns.
messages.action_open: Open the message from the queue.
messages.action_read: Read the full exchange — previous messages give context.
messages.action_mark: Click Mark as reviewed with optional notes, or escalate.
messages.action_escalate: If the message is a safeguarding concern, also create or update a guardian assignment in the Safeguarding dashboard.
messages.retention: Reviewed copies stay in the archive for 180 days then auto-purge (configurable in the Configuration sub-module).

monitoring.title: User monitoring & risk tags
monitoring.intro: There are two ways a member can end up on a watch list:
monitoring.automatic: Automatic (self-identified) — during onboarding, the member ticked a safeguarding option that has restricts_messaging or requires_broker_approval triggers. These are written to user_messaging_restrictions with reason "Safeguarding: self-identified during onboarding".
monitoring.manual: Manual (admin-initiated) — you open User Monitoring and add a member with a reason and optional expiry date. The reason is stored so any future reviewer knows why.
monitoring.expiry: Monitoring can be time-limited — set monitoring_expires_at and the daily safeguarding:clear-expired-monitoring command will silently lift the flag once the date passes.
monitoring.risk_tags: Risk tags are a listing-level flag, not a member-level one. Tag a listing if you want to watch how it performs without restricting the member.

vetting.intro: This page stores only the community's safeguarding decision. Do not upload or copy certificates, disclosure numbers, dates of birth, document references, results, or free-text evidence into NEXUS.
vetting.jurisdiction_england_wales: England and Wales: the supported contact policy uses a fixed Enhanced DBS attestation. A broker confirms only that the community completed its own checks for safeguarded member contact; NEXUS does not inspect or verify a certificate.
vetting.jurisdiction_elsewhere: Everywhere else: keep contact gating unavailable until a separate lawful policy has been designed and approved. Do not treat PVG, AccessNI, Garda vetting, or overseas checks as interchangeable with Enhanced DBS.
vetting.workflow_review: A member can request broker review. The request contains status and purpose metadata only; it is not an application or evidence upload.
vetting.workflow_confirm: A broker acknowledges the fixed statement and records the community decision as confirmed. No certificate file, number, date, result, or note is stored.
vetting.workflow_revoke: A broker can change the decision to revoked using a controlled reason code. Future messaging checks use the new decision immediately.
vetting.privacy: Never ask a member to send a vetting document through messages or upload it to this panel. Any lawful check and identity matching remains in the community's approved process outside NEXUS.
vetting.messaging: The messenger gate opens only when the sender has the exact confirmed attestation required by the recipient's active community policy. Missing, revoked, or unavailable policy data fails closed.

alerts.title: Safeguarding alerts & escalation
alerts.counts_incidents: Open high- or critical-severity volunteer safeguarding incidents.
alerts.escalate_abuse: Suspected abuse or exploitation: stop, document, and contact your Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL) before taking any user-facing action. Do not warn the member; do not delete messages. The audit trail is evidence.
alerts.escalate_child: Child protection concern: in Ireland, notify Tusla; in UK, notify the local authority children's services. The law requires this regardless of the platform's internal process.
alerts.escalate_attestation: Attestation concern: revoke the community decision with the appropriate controlled reason and follow your approved safeguarding process outside NEXUS. Do not request or upload evidence through the platform.

legal.title: Legal framework & compliance
legal.scope_disclaimer: Vetting eligibility and reuse depend on the jurisdiction, role, activity, workforce, and organisation. This panel is an operational aid, not legal advice; your safeguarding lead or legal adviser must approve the community process.
legal.current_scope: Current supported scope: the automated contact gate supports the England and Wales Enhanced DBS community attestation for the fixed purpose of safeguarded member contact.
legal.jurisdiction_limits: Other jurisdictions: Scotland, Northern Ireland, Ireland, and overseas schemes need their own policy and role or scope model. Selecting a country label must never silently make an unsupported scheme equivalent.
legal.data_minimisation: Data minimisation: NEXUS records the community's decision and controlled audit metadata only. Certificates, disclosure content, identifiers, and free-text evidence must not be stored in the platform.

data.title: Where the data lives & retention
data.monitoring_status: Monitoring status: user_messaging_restrictions. Cleared automatically when expiry passes.
data.message_copies: Message copies: broker_message_copies. 180-day auto-purge via weekly scheduled command.
data.vetting_confirmations: Community attestations: member_vetting_attestations. Stores the fixed purpose, scope, policy version, decision, actors, timestamps, and controlled reason codes — never certificate evidence.
data.vetting_review_requests: Review requests: safeguarding_vetting_review_requests. Stores the member, fixed purpose, status, and resolution metadata only.
data.user_prefs: Member safeguarding preferences: user_safeguarding_preferences. Revoked by the member from their settings; tombstoned, not deleted.
data.guardian_assignments: Guardian assignments: safeguarding_assignments. Revocation is soft — the audit trail is preserved.
data.attestation_audit: Attestation decision history: member_vetting_attestation_events. It is intentionally separate from the general activity feed and contains controlled decision metadata, not documents or broker notes.

contacts.title: Who to contact & when
contacts.technical: Technical issue with this panel — open a ticket with your platform administrator; include the URL and approximate timestamp.
contacts.safeguarding: Safeguarding concern about a member — your community's Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL), not the platform. The DSL decides whether to escalate to statutory services.
contacts.criminality: Suspected criminality — law enforcement (999 / 112 for immediate danger). The platform's audit trail will support any subsequent investigation.
contacts.policy_scope: Policy question — contact your tenant admin, safeguarding lead, or compliance officer. A country label does not enable contact gating; only a separately supported and approved jurisdiction policy can do that.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting common situations
troubleshooting.cant_message_attestation: "A member says they cannot message someone" — check the recipient's safeguarding preference, the community jurisdiction policy, and the sender's current contact attestation. The gate remains closed unless the policy explicitly returns an allowed decision.
troubleshooting.wrong_member: "The wrong member is flagged" — open User Monitoring, remove them manually. If it was the onboarding auto-flag, the member can also revoke their own preference from Settings → Safeguarding.
troubleshooting.vetting_review: "A vetting review is still pending" — open Contact Attestations and either confirm the fixed community decision, revoke it with a controlled reason, or resolve the request without changing the attestation. Do not ask the member for documents in NEXUS.
troubleshooting.no_copies: "Messages aren't being copied" — confirm the member has under_monitoring = 1 on user_messaging_restrictions. If the row is missing, add them via User Monitoring with a reason.

help.search_empty_hint: Try a different keyword, or clear the search to browse every topic.
